name: 西安
description: Xi'an, the ancient capital of China and starting point of the Silk Road, is home to one of the world's greatest archaeological finds - the Terracotta Army. With 3,100 years of history, it offers ancient city walls, the Muslim Quarter's vibrant street food, the Big Wild Goose Pagoda, and access to the western region's stunning landscapes.
highlights: Ancient City Wall - Best preserved in China
Big Wild Goose Pagoda - Tang dynasty landmark

climate.tips: Best visited in spring and autumn. Summers hot but less humid than coastal cities. Winters cold but dry with occasional snow. Sandstorms possible in spring. Dress in layers.

attractions.name: 秦始皇兵马俑
attractions.description: The massive underground army of over 8,000 life-sized terracotta warriors buried to protect China's first emperor in his afterlife.
attractions.tips: Arrive early to avoid crowds. Take official shuttle bus from train station. Audio guide essential for history. Combine with Huaqing Hot Springs.

attractions.name: 西安城墙
attractions.description: The most complete ancient city wall in China, dating from the Ming dynasty. Walk or bike the full 14km circuit.
attractions.tips: Bike rental at multiple gates. Start from South Gate for best photos. Best at sunset for city views.

attractions.name: 大雁塔
attractions.description: The iconic Buddhist pagoda built in 652 AD to hold Buddhist scriptures brought from India by the monk Xuanzang.
attractions.tips: Evening fountain shows spectacular (20:30). Combined with nearby Qinling Mountains day trip.

attractions.name: 回民街
attractions.description: A vibrant neighborhood of Hui Muslims featuring ancient mosques, traditional architecture, and famous street food.
attractions.tips: Try biangbiang面, roujiamo, and persimmon cakes. Walk through early morning or evening. Cameras welcome except in mosque.

attractions.name: 陕西历史博物馆
attractions.description: One of China's best provincial museums housing artifacts from the Zhou to Tang dynasties, including the earliest Chinese civilization finds.
attractions.tips: Reserve tickets weeks ahead on official app. Free audio guides limited. Morning visits better.

attractions.name: 华清池
attractions.description: An imperial hot spring resort famous for the love story of Emperor Xuanzong and Yang Guifei, with Tang dynasty palace ruins.
attractions.address: Huaqing Rd, Lintong District, 30km from Xi'an
highlights: The Romance of Two Emperors show
attractions.tips: Combine with Terracotta Army day trip. Evening 'The Long Song' show spectacular. Best in spring.

attractions.name: 钟楼
attractions.description: The largest and most intact bell tower in China, built in 1384 during the Ming dynasty.
attractions.tips: Best viewed from outside at night. Combined ticket with Drum Tower saves money. Metro access convenient.

attractions.name: 小雁塔
attractions.description: A 15-story Tang dynasty pagoda built in 706 AD, surviving multiple earthquakes. Adjacent to the Xi'an Museum.
attractions.tips: Less crowded than Big Wild Goose Pagoda. Great photos from the courtyard. Free entry with museum reservation.

attractions.name: 汉阳陵
attractions.description: The tomb complex of Emperor Jingdi of the Han dynasty with remarkable underground museum displaying hundreds of figurines.
attractions.tips: Less famous than Terracotta Army but equally impressive. Take bus 4 from Xi'an. Great for understanding Han dynasty.

attractions.name: 鼓楼
attractions.description: The historic drum tower opposite the Bell Tower, marking the center of the old city with nightly percussion performances.
attractions.tips: Combined ticket with Bell Tower recommended. Evening drum show at 20:30. Great location near Muslim Quarter.

attractions.name: 华山
attractions.description: One of China's Five Great Mountains, famous for its dangerous plank walk along narrow ledges and dramatic peaks. A test of courage.
attractions.tips: Take high-speed train from Xi'an North Station. Stay overnight for sunrise. Physical fitness required. Arrive early.

attractions.name: 大唐芙蓉园
attractions.description: The largest Tang dynasty cultural theme park in China, featuring traditional gardens, performances, and imperial architecture.
attractions.tips: Best visited in evening for light shows. Book show tickets separately. Spring and autumn ideal.

attractions.name: 大明宫国家遗址公园
attractions.description: The former imperial palace complex of the Tang dynasty, now a vast archaeological park with reconstructed palaces and museum.
attractions.address: 丹凤门内侧, 新城区自强东路
attractions.tips: Rent electric cart for large area. Evening light show at 20:30. Best with audio guide.

attractions.name: 永兴坊
attractions.description: A revitalized historical block famous for traditional Shaanxi snacks and intangible cultural heritage workshops.
attractions.tips: Come hungry to try many snacks. Best after 18:00 when lit up. Great for souvenirs.

attractions.name: 寒窑
attractions.description: A legendary cave dwelling where the love story of Wang Baochuan and Xue Pinggui unfolded, now a scenic area.
attractions.tips: Good for couples. Valentine's Day special events. Combined with Tang Paradise nearby.

attractions.name: 秦岭野生动物园
attractions.description: A large zoo in the foothills of Qinling Mountains housing rare animals from Shaanxi province and beyond.
attractions.tips: Take the safari train early. Half-day trip combined with Qinling Mountains. Bring sunscreen.

attractions.name: 乐华城
attractions.description: A large theme park complex with roller coasters, water rides, and family attractions.
attractions.tips: Book online for discounts. Summer water park popular. One day each park minimum.

attractions.name: 法门寺
attractions.description: Home to a finger bone relic of Buddha, one of the most sacred Buddhist sites in China with Tang dynasty architecture.
attractions.tips: About 120km from Xi'an - go early. Wear comfortable shoes. Buddhist vegetarian restaurant on site.

attractions.name: 西安碑林博物馆
attractions.description: The oldest stone inscription museum in China, housing famous calligraphy works and ancient steles.
attractions.tips: Essential for calligraphy enthusiasts. Audio guide recommended. Combine with City Wall walk.

attractions.name: 曲江海洋馆
attractions.description: A large aquarium with marine performances, tropical fish exhibits, and a small theme park.
attractions.tips: Check show schedules on arrival. Good for rainy days. Combine with Tang Paradise in same area.

attractions.name: 终南山
attractions.description: The sacred Taoist mountain known as the 'Divine Mountain', featuring ancient temples and stunning peaks.
attractions.tips: Take cable car up, hike down. Physical fitness required for summit. Best in autumn.

attractions.name: 大兴善寺
attractions.description: One of the oldest and most significant Buddhist temples in China, originally built during the Sui dynasty.
attractions.address: 雁塔区小寨兴善寺西街
attractions.tips: Very peaceful, less touristy. Peonies in April spectacular. Good for meditation.

attractions.name: 骊山国家森林公园
attractions.description: The scenic mountain near Huaqing Palace, famous for its terraces, pavilions, and panoramic views of Xi'an.
attractions.tips: Combine with Huaqing Hot Springs. Take cable car. Sunset visits spectacular. Spring for flowers.

attractions.name: 寒舍遗址公园
attractions.description: A park recreating the legendary love story setting with gardens, sculptures, and performances.
attractions.tips: Good for couples. Combine with Han Yao scenic area. Evening lighting beautiful.

attractions.name: 咸阳湖
attractions.description: A scenic lake area with gardens, walking paths, and the historic Liumin Bridge.
attractions.tips: Best at sunset. Combine with nearby museums. Great for evening strolls.

attractions.name: 霸陵
attractions.description: The tomb of Emperor Wen of Han, known for his benevolent rule and simple burial customs.
attractions.tips: Less visited than other tombs. Good for history enthusiasts. Combine with nearby Baqiao.

attractions.name: 秦岭分水岭
attractions.description: The natural boundary between north and south China, with stunning mountain scenery and forests.
attractions.tips: Take guided tour for safety. Best in summer for escape from heat. Bring layers for high altitude.

attractions.name: 咸阳博物馆
attractions.description: Museum showcasing the history of the Qin's capital and Han dynasty imperial artifacts.
attractions.tips: Combine with visit to Xianyang Lake. Good for understanding Qin history before Terracotta Army.

attractions.name: 洛阳龙门石窟
attractions.description: One of China's three greatest Buddhist grotto complexes with thousands of carved figures.
attractions.tips: About 250km from Xi'an - best as overnight trip. Morning visits best for photos. Comfortable shoes essential.

restaurants.name: 长安大牌档
restaurants.description: One Michelin star restaurant serving excellent Shaanxi cuisine with traditional atmosphere and folk performances.

restaurants.name: 遇见长安
restaurants.description: Highly-rated Shaanxi restaurant with authentic flavors and local specialties.

restaurants.name: 老孙家
restaurants.description: Legendary Muslim restaurant known for excellent lamb dishes and roujiamo.

restaurants.name: 德发长
restaurants.description: Famous for making over 100 varieties of dumplings for centuries.

restaurants.name: 春发生
restaurants.description: Historic restaurant serving traditional Xi'an breakfast and offal dishes.

restaurants.name: 花腰崽
restaurants.description: One diamond Black Pearl restaurant serving refined Shaanxi cuisine in elegant Tang dynasty styled interior.

restaurants.name: 贾三清真灌汤包
restaurants.description: The most famous soup dumpling (xiaolongbao) place in the Muslim Quarter, always packed.

restaurants.name: 老刘家泡馍
restaurants.description: Legendary restaurant for authentic Yangrou Pao Mo - hand-torn flatbread in lamb broth.
dishHighlights: Yangrou pao mo (lamb broth with torn bread)

restaurants.name: biangbiang面
restaurants.description: Student-favorite spot serving the famous wide belt noodles with spicy sauce, cheap and authentic.

restaurants.name: 东南亚甑糕
restaurants.description: Famous Muslim Quarter dessert vendor selling jing gao (steamed glutinous rice with red beans and dates) for decades.

restaurants.name: 同盛祥
restaurants.description: A century-old restaurant specializing in authentic Yangrou Pao Mo with hand-torn bread technique.

restaurants.name: 海荣锅贴
restaurants.description: Famous for crispy pan-fried dumplings (guotie) with juicy fillings, a Xi'an street food staple.

restaurants.name: 魏家凉皮
restaurants.description: Popular chain serving cold skin noodles (liangpi) with secret spicy sauce, refreshing and delicious.

restaurants.name: 子午路肉夹馍
restaurants.description: Legendary roujiamo shop with perfectly braised pork in crispy buns, a Xi'an must-try breakfast.

restaurants.name: 张记肉夹馍
restaurants.description: Another famous roujiamo spot known for generous portions of braised pork and fresh-baked buns.

restaurants.name: 马虎面
restaurants.description: Legendary breakfast spot famous for hand-pulled noodles and crispy roujiamo, closes early.

restaurants.name: 柳巷面
restaurants.description: Famous for knife-cut noodles with rich tomato egg sauce, a local favorite for decades.

restaurants.name: 郑大妈biangbiang面
restaurants.description: Highly-rated spot for the famous wide belt noodles with mixed toppings and spicy oil.

restaurants.name: 定家小酥肉
restaurants.description: Iconic Muslim Quarter restaurant known for crispy fried lamb ribs and rice dishes.

restaurants.name: 稀稀锅盔
restaurants.description: Traditional bakery famous for giant crispy flatbread (guokui), perfect with lamb soup.

restaurants.name: 陕十三
restaurants.description: Black Pearl restaurant offering modern takes on traditional Shaanxi dishes in elegant setting.

restaurants.name: 长安大排档
restaurants.description: Popular chain offering variety of Shaanxi small dishes in lively dapai档 atmosphere.

restaurants.name: 西安饭庄
restaurants.description: Historic Shaanxi restaurant established in 1929, famous for traditional dishes.

restaurants.name: 老味道
restaurants.description: Restaurant specializing in traditional Shaanxi home-style dishes.

restaurants.name: 天下第一面
restaurants.description: Popular for variety of traditional Shaanxi noodles in generous portions.

restaurants.name: 小杨烤肉
restaurants.description: Popular Muslim Quarter BBQ restaurant with fresh lamb and traditional marinades.

restaurants.name: 大厨小馆
restaurants.description: Restaurant offering refined Shaanxi cuisine with artistic presentation.

restaurants.name: 西北饭庄
restaurants.description: Popular for northwestern Chinese cuisine with large portions.

restaurants.name: 醉西安
restaurants.description: Modern restaurant with creative Shaanxi dishes and craft beer.

restaurants.name: 粉巷13号
restaurants.description: Popular spot for traditional Shaanxi breakfast and snacks.

arrival.from: Major domestic airports, growing international routes
arrival.tips: Airport metro line connects to city center. Consider high-speed trains from many cities - often more convenient.
High-speed trains fastest and most comfortable. Xi'an has multiple stations - check destination carefully.

metro: Lines 2 and 4 most useful for tourists
bus: Download 'Xi'an Metro' app for English info
taxi: Didi app works well in English
Airport taxi counters official and reliable
bike: Not as common as coastal cities

hotels.name: 西安香格里拉大酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Request higher floors for city views.

hotels.name: 西安丽思卡尔顿酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best for business travelers, slightly removed from old town.

hotels.name: 西安喜来登大酒店
hotels.address: No. 12, Feng Hao Rd, Xincheng District

hotels.name: 西安印家花园酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Book courtyard rooms for unique experience.

hotels.name: 西安威斯汀酒店
hotels.address: No. 66 Ci En Rd, Yanta District

hotels.name: 西安希尔顿酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best for travelers arriving by train.

hotels.name: 西安jw万豪酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Book executive rooms for lounge access.

hotels.name: 西安全季酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Good balance of price and quality.

tips: Xi'an is tourist-friendly - many places accept cards
But Alipay still essential for markets and small vendors
Both apps equally useful in Xi'an

payment.description: Useful for traditional markets and small vendors
howToUse: Keep small bills for street food
tips: Tourist sites have official price boards

culturalTips.title: Shaanxi Cuisine
culturalTips.content: Xi'an is the birthplace of many famous Chinese foods including biangbiang面, roujiamo, and lamb paomo. Muslim Quarter offers unique Hui (Chinese Muslim) cuisine.

culturalTips.title: Modest Dress for Relics
culturalTips.content: When visiting religious sites and Terracotta Army, dress modestly and respectfully. Shoulders and knees covered.

culturalTips.title: Historical Context Matters
culturalTips.content: Xi'an has been capital of multiple dynasties. Understanding Chinese history enhances the experience. Audio guides recommended.

culturalTips.title: Night at the Wall
culturalTips.content: Xian's city wall is spectacular at night with the giant music fountain show at the South Gate. The nearby Muslim Quarter comes alive in the evening with hundreds of food stalls selling roujiamo, lamb skewers, and biangbiang noodles.

culturalTips.title: Silk Road Heritage
culturalTips.content: Xi'an was the eastern terminus of the ancient Silk Road. The Grand Mosque of Xi'an reflects the city's historical connections with Central Asia and the Islamic world.

culturalTips.title: Terracotta Army Significance
culturalTips.content: The Terracotta Army represents Emperor Qin Shi Huang's quest for immortality. Each warrior has unique facial features - no two are exactly alike.

culturalTips.title: Hui Muslim Culture
culturalTips.content: The Muslim Quarter (Huimin Jie) is home to Xi'an's Hui Muslim community who have lived here for over 1,000 years. They follow Islamic dietary laws - no pork.

culturalTips.title: Noodle Culture
culturalTips.content: Shaanxi people take noodles very seriously. Biangbiang面 (belt noodles) are a local specialty - the character for 'biang' is one of the most complex Chinese characters.

culturalTips.title: Qin Dynasty Legacy
culturalTips.content: Xi'an (as Chang'an) was the capital of the Qin dynasty that first unified China. The Terracotta Army, city walls, and historical sites reflect this legacy.

culturalTips.title: Tang Dynasty Golden Age
culturalTips.content: The Tang dynasty (618-907 AD) is considered China's golden age. Xi'an was the largest city in the world, with cosmopolitan culture influences from the Silk Road.

culturalTips.title: Cycling the City Wall
culturalTips.content: Cycling the 14km City Wall circuit is a Xi'an tradition. Rent bikes at South Gate or other gates. Best done at sunset for city views.

culturalTips.title: Local Dialect
culturalTips.content: Xi'an dialect has its own characteristics, including the suffix 'de' (嘚) at sentence end. Many locals still use traditional pronunciation for certain characters.

culturalTips.title: Dumpling Banquet
culturalTips.content: Xi'an is famous for dumpling (jiaozi) restaurants. Try the dumpling banquet at De Fa Chang - a single meal can include 100+ varieties.

culturalTips.title: Tea Culture
culturalTips.content: While not as tea-centric as southern China, Xi'an has its own tea traditions. Locals enjoy herbal teas and the famous Faban lotus leaf tea.

culturalTips.title: Foot Massage Culture
culturalTips.content: Like many Chinese cities, Xi'an has many foot massage shops. After a day of walking the Terracotta Army or City Wall, a foot massage is welcome relief.

culturalTips.title: Historical Superstitions
culturalTips.content: Traditional beliefs about spirits and ancestors are still present. Don't point at the moon (disrespectful to Moon God), and avoid cutting hair on certain festival days.

culturalTips.title: Local Festivals
culturalTips.content: During Chinese New Year, Xi'an's Muslim Quarter has special foods. The Drums and Bells Festival celebrates ancient music traditions.

culturalTips.title: Ancient Architecture
culturalTips.content: Xi'an's architecture reflects multiple dynasties. The Bell and Drum Towers (Ming dynasty) are iconic. Han and Tang dynasties left their mark on palace ruins.

culturalTips.title: Hot Spring Culture
culturalTips.content: 华清池 (Huaqing Hot Springs) has been a resort since Tang dynasty. Emperor Xuanzong built a palace here for his favorite consort Yang Guifei.

culturalTips.title: Traditional Handicrafts
culturalTips.content: Xi'an is known for traditional crafts including shadow puppets, paper cutting, and glazed pottery. The Tang Dynasty crafts make unique souvenirs.

culturalTips.title: Imperial Tomb Culture
culturalTips.content: Xi'an is surrounded by imperial tombs from multiple dynasties. The area northwest of the city has 9 of the 73 Tang dynasty imperial tombs.

emergencyContacts.name: 西安交通大学第一附属医院
emergencyContacts.nameEn: First Affiliated Hospital of Xi'an Jiaotong University
emergencyContacts.notes: Top public hospital, excellent for complex cases

emergencyContacts.name: 美国驻西安总领事馆
emergencyContacts.address: 1st Floor, Sheraton Xi'an Hotel, 12 Feng Hao Rd
emergencyContacts.notes: Limited consular services - may need to travel to Beijing or Shanghai

emergencyContacts.name: 英国驻西安总领事馆
emergencyContacts.address: 4th Floor, Xin Hua Hotel, 115 South Second Ring Rd

emergencyContacts.name: 日本驻西安总领事馆
emergencyContacts.address: 1st Floor, Xi'an Credit Hotel, 88 South Guangji Rd

emergencyContacts.name: 西安旅游投诉热线
emergencyContacts.notes: Report issues with tour guides, attractions, or services

quickFacts.visa: Most nationalities require visa. 72h transit visa available at airport for eligible nationalities.
quickFacts.water: Tap water safe but use bottled for taste
